because the person is better prepared now for the sales event, in this case, the closing call on the phone, and so conversion percentages go up, the stress of the close goes down, buyers remorse after the sale, and you may not be having any of these issues if you aren't, you aren't. But so many times the delayed sale, while uncomfortable for everybody involved in it, because we all, you know, they raise their hand, let's jump on them before they change their mind, kind of thing, but often the delayed sale's the better sale, all right? Even to the point that it can affect how they behave after the sale. So, so that's an unknown corollary question to the first question. The way some beat it is that there's a, there's some sort of first conversation at which point about them getting sent the stuff, and there is an agreement to review the stuff before the phone appointment that is scheduled before the stuff is sent, okay? In some environments, so I've done it with three clients, the, there's a deposit required for the stuff. So when we move people from, say, a physical discovery day, right? So process one is they're coming to a discovery day, at the end of the day, they're either gonna join, they're not gonna join, yada, yada, yada, right? So then we switch that to the discovery day comes to you, right? But that phone conversation is about, okay, the discovery day's good, you gotta give us the same $1,500 deposit you were given to come to discovery day, and it works fundamentally the same way. And the real world and the physical world, at the end of the day, if you decide no, you get your grant and a half back. In some cases, you get your airfare refunded, whatever, and you go home, right? In this case, you get the $1,500 back when you return the discovery day in a box, and you come to your phone appointment. So now they have four days, five days, they have what was ever agreed upon to look at the stuff. Okay, so that's how some people get around it. And then the other way you can help them is shortening this timeframe after the first call, the number of calls that are required, you know, to make that sale. In given that you, but you have big numbers. I mean, you're niche, what's your total niche universe size? Well, if you, new card's 25,000, and then with used cards another 50,000. Yeah, okay. So you're almost mainstream, I mean, with that size audience. So it matters, cause guys can only do so many calls, right? And so if it's taken them, let's say nine calls, right, to close a sale, that's in the way of them talking to more prospects. And so you reach a point of your growth now requires you to keep putting more phone guys in a room. And of course, the more you put in there, the next three are gonna be even less effective than the three you got now. So now if it takes these three, six calls to close, it's gonna take them nine. And the next, and by the way, they're gonna affect the ones that are closing in six. So pretty soon everybody's gonna take nine. And then the next three you put in there, in between drooling, it's gonna take them four to, you know, so it matters, right? And so I definitely would be experimenting. And I think I'd be trying to do both. I think I would be trying to influence this before they were on the phone, and influence this after they were on the phone. And I think the way I would do the before experiment, and obviously you can run it with a small number of leads and kind of know what happens, is I think I would require them to swear that they had read, listened to, watched, whatever you decide to do before they're allowed to make that phone, before they're allowed to be on the phone. And that rep, so Charlie now gets on the phone, he says, Charlie, did you read the book? No, I didn't get around to it. So you could try and force it. But yeah, I would be trying to do it because I think you can make your guys more productive. And just shortening the sales cycle has monetary impact in the business. Yeah, the other thing to remember that you're gonna wanna try in there is you're gonna wanna try a handoff. You are gonna wanna develop some mechanism by which the guy Charlie can't close by the fifth call gets switched to Bob, and the guy Bob can't close by the fifth call gets switched to Charlie. Mike, we're gonna get everybody that's here in one more. We're doing something different, which falls under the opportunity setting. We have a, I'm working with a non-practicing dentist, and he has a great story, basically sold his company, leveraged real estate to get into that. One of the things we're looking at is setting it up so we teach other people how to have an exit strategy to get out of their business, which is basically opportunity. And we're focusing on helping other professionals create that exit for them, and quite honestly, we have some systems that he's used in his business to systematize it, so he didn't have to be there as much because of family issues and things. He had to be away from the business a lot, and so that worked out well. So you kind of caught my interest yesterday, because originally I had planned on us reaching out to some of the professionals.
